sourceMovie.title: Venom
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Venom for the same reason it broke through in 2018: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a action rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Ruben Fleischer keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The science fiction and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Avengers
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Venom clicked for you, The Avengers (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Venom clicked for you, Avengers: Infinity War (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Venom?
faq.answer: Try The Avengers, Avengers: Infinity War, Guardians of the Galaxy—each shares DNA with Venom while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Venom?
faq.answer: Venom (2018) is best described as Science Fiction, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Venom part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Venom Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
